title: quillajaceae
intro: The quillajaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants. Quillajaceae is a small family of plants, commonly known as soapbark trees or quillai trees, native to South America. It consists of approximately 10 species, primarily found in the Andes mountain range. The quillajaceae family is characterized by its unique bark, which is used to produce a soap-like substance, and its clusters of small, white or yellow flowers.
